newsDiscoveryHints.geographicHint: NC-01 spans 24 eastern North Carolina counties/partial-counties anchored by Nash and Edgecombe (Rocky Mount) and stretching to the Outer Banks (Dare, Currituck) and Elizabeth City (Pasquotank); redrawn by SB 249 / SL 2025-95 in October 2025.

src-nc-sb747.name: North Carolina Senate Bill 747 (2023) — Voter ID / No Drop Boxes
src-nc-sb747.notes: In effect since 2023, confirmed for 2026. Requires photo ID, bans absentee drop boxes, and eliminates the post-Election-Day mail-receipt grace period for domestic ballots.

src-nc-sb656.notes: Lowered the second-primary (runoff) trigger threshold from 40% to 30% of the vote.

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: North Carolina's 2026 statewide primary. Don Davis (D) advanced uncontested; Laurie Buckhout (R) won a 5-way Republican primary with 39.52% (26,624 votes), beating Asa Buck (34.45%). Polls open 6:30 a.m.-7:30 p.m.
ELECTION_EVENTS.governingAuthority: North Carolina State Board of Elections

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: A second (runoff) primary is triggered only if the leading primary candidate receives less than 30% of the vote (lowered from 40% by SB 656 in 2017), and only if requested by the second-place finisher within 9 days of the primary. Not triggered in NC-01: Buckhout's 39.52% cleared the threshold.
ELECTION_EVENTS.governingAuthority: North Carolina State Board of Elections

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Last day to register online or by mail for the November 3, 2026 general election, 5:00 p.m. (25 days before Election Day). Same-day registration remains available in person during the early-voting window.
ELECTION_EVENTS.governingAuthority: North Carolina State Board of Elections

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Early in-person voting begins statewide, including all NC-01 counties. Same-day voter registration is available in person during this window for voters who missed the standard registration deadline (they must provide proof of residence).
ELECTION_EVENTS.governingAuthority: North Carolina State Board of Elections

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Deadline to submit a mail absentee ballot request for the November 3, 2026 general election, 5:00 p.m.
ELECTION_EVENTS.governingAuthority: North Carolina State Board of Elections

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Last day of early in-person voting, closing at 3:00 p.m. on the final Saturday before Election Day.
ELECTION_EVENTS.governingAuthority: North Carolina State Board of Elections

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: U.S. midterm general election. NC-01: Don Davis (D, inc.) vs. Laurie Buckhout (R), a rematch of the 2024 race. Polls open 6:30 a.m.-7:30 p.m. Administered under the new NC-01 boundaries drawn by SB 249 / SL 2025-95 (Oct. 2025); a federal three-judge panel denied a preliminary injunction against the map on Nov. 26, 2025, and plaintiffs dropped their appeal in January 2026.
ELECTION_EVENTS.governingAuthority: North Carolina State Board of Elections

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Absentee ballots must be received (not merely postmarked) by the county board of elections by 7:30 p.m. on Election Day. North Carolina eliminated its post-Election-Day mail-receipt grace period for domestic ballots under SB 747 (2023); UOCAVA (military/overseas) ballots retain a longer receipt window.
ELECTION_EVENTS.governingAuthority: North Carolina State Board of Elections

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Voters who cast a provisional ballot due to lacking accepted photo ID must cure it — by providing ID or completing an ID Exception Form — by noon on the day before the county canvass (noon, Nov. 12, 2026, ahead of the Nov. 13 canvass).
County boards of elections meet at 11:00 a.m. to canvass and certify county-level results, including provisional and cured ballots.
The North Carolina State Board of Elections meets at 11:00 a.m. to certify final statewide results, including the NC-01 general election outcome.
ELECTION_EVENTS.governingAuthority: North Carolina State Board of Elections

RULES.currentRule: Photo ID is required to vote in person and by mail, per SB 747 (2023), in effect since 2023 and confirmed for 2026. Accepted forms: NC driver's license or non-driver ID, another state's driver's license (if registered to vote within 90 days of the election), U.S. passport, military/veteran ID, tribal enrollment card, an NCSBE-approved student or employee ID, or a free NC voter photo ID from the county board of elections. A voter without acceptable ID casts a provisional ballot and must cure it by providing ID or completing an ID Exception Form by noon on the day before the county canvass.
RULES.whyItMatters: NC-01 voters across all 24 counties/partial-counties must present qualifying photo ID at the polls or with their absentee ballot. Voters lacking ID have a narrow but workable cure window (through noon on Nov. 12, 2026 for the general election) to avoid having their provisional ballot rejected.
RULES.detail: SB 747 (2023). Cure deadline: noon on the day before county canvass (noon Nov. 12, 2026, ahead of the Nov. 13 canvass).

RULES.currentRule: North Carolina does not allow absentee ballot drop boxes. SB 747 (2023) explicitly bans them statewide; ballots must be returned by mail or in person to the county board of elections office.
RULES.whyItMatters: NC-01 voters cannot rely on a drop box network for absentee ballot return, unlike some other states. Ballots must be mailed (allowing enough time to be received by the deadline) or delivered in person to the county board of elections office.
RULES.detail: SB 747 (2023). Confirmed by multiple county board-of-elections sources per research; no dedicated NC-01 drop-box resource tile exists as a result.

RULES.topic: Absentee ballot receipt deadline — no postmark grace period (SB 747)
RULES.currentRule: Absentee ballots must be received (not merely postmarked) by the county board of elections by 7:30 p.m. on Election Day (Nov. 3, 2026). SB 747 (2023) eliminated North Carolina's post-Election-Day mail-receipt grace period for domestic ballots; UOCAVA (military/overseas) ballots retain a longer receipt window.
RULES.whyItMatters: NC-01 voters mailing an absentee ballot must ensure it physically arrives by 7:30 p.m. on Election Day — a postmark on or before Election Day is not sufficient for domestic ballots. Voters mailing close to Election Day should consider in-person return instead.
RULES.detail: SB 747 (2023). Applies statewide, including all NC-01 counties.

RULES.currentRule: Since SB 656 (2017), a second (runoff) primary is triggered only if the leading primary candidate receives less than 30% of the vote (previously 40%), and only if requested by the second-place finisher within 9 days of the primary. Not triggered in NC-01's 2026 primary — Buckhout won with 39.52%, comfortably above the threshold.
RULES.whyItMatters: NC-01's Republican primary produced a clear nominee (Buckhout) without a runoff. This rule is relevant background for understanding how NC-01's general-election matchup (Davis vs. Buckhout) was finalized.
RULES.detail: SB 656 (2017). NEEDS REVIEW: exact hour-of-day for the second-primary request deadline (9 days after the March 3 primary) was described generally per MultiState and not independently re-confirmed against NCSBE's own second-primary procedures page.

RULES.currentRule: North Carolina's congressional map was redrawn via SB 249 / Session Law 2025-95, enacted October 2025, altering NC-01 and NC-03 boundaries (NC-11 was left unchanged). A federal three-judge panel denied a preliminary injunction against the new map on November 26, 2025, and plaintiffs dropped their appeal in January 2026. The map is locked in with no pending litigation risk for the November 3, 2026 general election.
RULES.whyItMatters: NC-01 voters are voting under new district boundaries for the 2026 cycle. Voters near the district's edges (Gates, Pasquotank, Perquimans, Granville, or Bibb-adjacent areas) should confirm their correct district assignment via the NCSBE polling lookup tool.
RULES.detail: NEEDS REVIEW: the enacted SL 2025-95 plan PDF was not independently re-fetched from ncleg.gov in this pass; corroborated via multiple news/legal-tracker sources instead. Gates, Pasquotank, and Perquimans partial-county split-boundary details were confirmed as partial via Wikipedia only, not an NCSBE/county GIS source.

RULES.topic: Same-day registration during early voting only
RULES.currentRule: North Carolina allows same-day voter registration only during the early-voting (One-Stop) period, from Oct. 15 through Oct. 31, 2026, for the general election. Voters must provide proof of residence. Standard online/mail registration closes 25 days before Election Day (Oct. 9, 2026, 5:00 p.m.); there is no same-day registration on Election Day itself at Election Day precincts.
RULES.whyItMatters: NC-01 voters who miss the Oct. 9 registration deadline have a narrower path than in some other states: they can still register and vote during the Oct. 15-31 early-voting window with proof of residence, but not on Election Day itself.
RULES.detail: NEEDS REVIEW: this rule reflects the research brief's instruction to verify same-day registration availability against current law; the underlying NCSBE same-day registration procedure page was not independently re-fetched in this pass to confirm the exact proof-of-residence document list.

RESOURCES.governingAuthority: North Carolina State Board of Elections

RESOURCES.label: Find voting locations
RESOURCES.description: Official North Carolina polling place lookup tool, including Election Day precincts and early-voting (One-Stop) sites.

RESOURCES.label: Find ballot boxes
RESOURCES.description: North Carolina does not allow absentee ballot drop boxes. SB 747 (2023) bans them statewide; ballots must be returned by mail or in person to the county board of elections office. Use the county directory to find your NC-01 county's office.
RESOURCES.governingAuthority: North Carolina State Board of Elections (statutory prohibition); county boards of elections (in-person return locations)

RESOURCES.label: Check registration status
RESOURCES.description: Official North Carolina voter registration lookup tool — confirm your registration status and voting history.
RESOURCES.governingAuthority: North Carolina State Board of Elections

RESOURCES.label: Track your ballot
RESOURCES.description: Official North Carolina absentee ballot tracker (BallotTrax integration) — check when your mail ballot was sent, received, and accepted.
RESOURCES.governingAuthority: North Carolina State Board of Elections

RESOURCES.label: Voter ID information

RESOURCES.governingAuthority: North Carolina State Board of Elections

UPDATES.title: NC-01 dashboard initialized from wa-03 template — 20 sources, 11 events, 6 rules wired
UPDATES.newValue: Initial build July 9, 2026, cloned from the WA-03 template structure. All 20 sources (NCSBE main/register/polling-lookup/reg-lookup/vote-by-mail/voter-ID/results/county-directory, Guilford and Iredell county elections, SB 249/SL 2025-95, SB 747, SB 656, MultiState, ABC11, Carolina Journal x2, News & Observer, PBS NewsHour, Wikipedia) recorded from primary-source research. All 11 election events and 6 voting rules researched from NCSBE and statutory sources, but all marked needsReview/unconfirmed pending human QA. Candidates: Don Davis (D, inc.) vs. Laurie Buckhout (R), a rematch of the 2024 race.

UPDATES.title: SB 249 / SL 2025-95 redrew NC-01 boundaries; litigation resolved with no pending risk
UPDATES.oldValue: NC-01 boundaries as used in the 2024 election, prior to the October 2025 redistricting act.
UPDATES.newValue: SB 249 / Session Law 2025-95 (enacted October 2025) redrew NC-01 and NC-03 boundaries. A federal three-judge panel denied a preliminary injunction against the new map on November 26, 2025, and plaintiffs dropped their appeal in January 2026 — the map is locked in for the November 3, 2026 general election with no pending litigation risk.

UPDATES.title: All rules and events flagged for county-level and legislative-text verification
UPDATES.newValue: 6 voting rules and 11 election events all marked needsReview: true, confidence: unconfirmed (lastVerified 2026-07-09). Verify individual NC-01 county board-of-elections contact details against vt.ncsbe.gov/boeinfo/, confirm the enacted SL 2025-95 plan PDF directly from ncleg.gov, and confirm the same-day registration proof-of-residence document list before publish.

NEWS.headline: Live updates: North Carolina 2026 primary election day is underway
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Reports Laurie Buckhout's win in the NC-01 5-way Republican primary with 39.52% of the vote, setting up a rematch against incumbent Don Davis (D) in the November general election.

NEWS.headline: Davis outpaces challenger Buckhout in NC-1 fundraising
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Reports on the financial dynamics of the Davis-Buckhout NC-01 general-election rematch ahead of November 2026.

NEWS.headline: NC congressional map critics drop federal lawsuits
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Confirms plaintiffs dropped their appeal challenging the SB 249 / SL 2025-95 congressional map, removing the last litigation risk to NC-01's redrawn boundaries for the 2026 general election.

NEWS.headline: Judges allow North Carolina to use a map drawn in bid to give Republicans another U.S. House seat
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Reports the federal three-judge panel's denial of a preliminary injunction against North Carolina's new congressional map, which redrew NC-01 and NC-03.

LAST_REFRESH.summary: Initial WA-03-cloned build for NC-01, populated from primary-source research (NCSBE, SB 249/SL 2025-95, SB 747, SB 656, Guilford/Iredell county elections, and dated news reporting). All election events and voting rules remain marked needsReview: true and confidence: unconfirmed pending human QA, including confirmation of individual county contact details and the enacted redistricting plan PDF.

WEEKLY_REPORT.summary: Initial build for NC-01, cloned from the WA-03 dashboard template ahead of the November 3, 2026 general election. This is a rematch: Don Davis (D, inc.) vs. Laurie Buckhout (R), who won a 5-way GOP primary on March 3, 2026 with 39.52% (above the 30% second-primary threshold, so no runoff was triggered). NC-01's boundaries were redrawn by SB 249 / SL 2025-95 (Oct. 2025); litigation was resolved with no pending risk after plaintiffs dropped their appeal in January 2026. Statewide-uniform general-election calendar: registration deadline Oct 9, early voting Oct 15-31 (with same-day registration only during this window), absentee request deadline Oct 20, Election Day Nov 3 (7:30 p.m. absentee receipt deadline, no postmark grace period per SB 747), ID-cure deadline noon Nov 12, county canvass Nov 13, state canvass Nov 24. North Carolina bans absentee drop boxes statewide (SB 747). All 20 sources verified as of July 9, 2026. All events and rules flagged needsReview pending county-specific and legislative-text confirmation.
